completion_criteria_checked: A local deterministic script validates the three canonical files against their schemas, reporting pass/fail per file with a human-readable reason.
doctor.ps1 composes this as one additional advisory finding (OK/ISSUE), continuing to always exit 0.
The schema-check script never gates, blocks, or fails any other script or cycle.
The check correctly passes on live files and correctly fails on deliberate violations.
Change stays in V1 scope, remains local deterministic, no broad orchestration/automation or hard gate.
Claude returns evidence in .cockpit/result/worker-result.md using the required format.
evidence_reviewed: grep -rn check-schemas across scripts/*.ps1 (confirmed only referenced by doctor.ps1, never called elsewhere or treated as a gate)
independent live-repo run of doctor.ps1 confirming the Format check finding is OK and total finding count is correct

independent scratch-copy reproduction using a different corruption than the worker tested (missing required field 'current_phase' in project-state.json, vs. the worker's enum/additionalProperties tests), confirming ISSUE detection and exit code 0
risks: check-schemas.ps1 requires pwsh; if unavailable, doctor.ps1 falls back to a generic ISSUE rather than real schema status - an inherited risk already accepted for the other composed checks.
Test-Json's error messages use JSON Pointer paths (e.g. '/task_status') rather than plain English, less immediately readable than doctor's other prose findings.
handoff-packet.schema.json was deliberately excluded since no live JSON runtime file exists to validate against it.

Self-verification note (DECISION-019): mitigated by independently reproducing the ISSUE path with a different schema violation (missing required field) than either of the worker's own tests (bad enum, disallowed additional property), and by confirming via grep that the new script is never invoked outside doctor.ps1.
summary: Independently re-ran doctor.ps1 against the live repo (confirmed OK) and reproduced the ISSUE path in a fresh scratch copy using a missing-required-field violation, different from either corruption the worker tested, confirming the check genuinely generalizes rather than being tuned to the worker's specific test cases. Confirmed via grep that check-schemas.ps1 is referenced only by doctor.ps1, never wired as a gate. This closes out IDEA-003, the last open V1 backlog item. All completion criteria met; no out-of-scope changes found.
next_action: Task 'pcc-v1-015' is complete and verified PASS. This closes the last open V1 backlog item; owner and advisor should formally wrap V1 and transition to the next phase (BRR - babysitter reduction role - Phase 1).
